questions.text: Why is this Surah a strong proof against arrogance and denial?
questions.answer: It declares the downfall of a prominent enemy, showing Allah’s knowledge and truth of revelation.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches family ties to righteous people guarantee salvation.
questions.answer: False. Abu Lahab was a close relative yet condemned due to disbelief and hostility.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about relying on wealth for security?
questions.answer: Wealth cannot protect from Allah’s judgment; it can become a source of delusion.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention both Abu Lahab and his wife?
questions.answer: To show that partnership in sin and hostility shares consequences.

questions.text: What is one moral lesson from 'the carrier of firewood'?
questions.answer: Those who spread harm and stir hostility carry the fuel of their own punishment.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that someone can oppose truth without spiritual consequences if they are influential.
questions.answer: False. Influence does not shield from Allah’s judgment.

questions.text: What does the ‘rope of palm fiber’ symbolize about punishment?
questions.answer: Humiliation, restraint, and fitting consequences for harmful behavior.

questions.text: How can this Surah guide a believer facing insults for their faith?
questions.answer: It reassures that Allah sees hostility and will judge; remain patient and firm.

questions.text: What is one modern example of ‘hindering Allah’s message’?
questions.answer: Mocking Islam publicly, spreading lies about it, or pressuring others to abandon worship.

questions.text: How does this Surah teach personal responsibility despite social environment?
questions.answer: Even in a family close to the Prophet, a person is judged by faith and deeds.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that repentance is impossible for enemies of truth.
questions.answer: False. Repentance is always possible before death, but persistent hostility leads to ruin.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Masad' mean?
questions.answer: The Palm Fiber / The twisted rope.

questions.text: Who is the primary person condemned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Abu Lahab (the uncle of the Prophet SAW).

questions.text: What does 'Tabbat' mean in the first verse?
questions.answer: Perished, ruined, or destroyed.

questions.text: What was Abu Lahab's real name?
questions.answer: Abd al-Uzza bin Abdul-Muttalib.

questions.text: What was the 'crime' of Abu Lahab mentioned in Hadith?
questions.answer: He was the most vocal and insulting opponent of the Prophet (SAW) when he first called the Quraysh to Islam on Mount Safa.

questions.text: Will Abu Lahab's wealth or children benefit him in this Surah?
questions.answer: No, they will not avail him.

questions.text: Where will Abu Lahab be thrown according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Into a Fire of blazing flame (Naran dhata lahab).

questions.text: Who else is condemned in this Surah?
questions.answer: His wife (Umm Jamil).

questions.text: How is Abu Lahab's wife described?
questions.answer: As the 'carrier of firewood' (Hammalatal hatab).

questions.text: What will be around the wife's neck?
questions.answer: A rope of twisted palm fiber (Hablum-mim-masad).

questions.text: Why was she called the 'carrier of firewood'?
questions.answer: Because she used to place thorny bushes in the Prophet's path to harm him, or it refers to her carrying wood for the fire of Hell.

questions.text: What is the miracle of this Surah regarding Abu Lahab's fate?
questions.answer: It predicted he would die as a disbeliever. He lived for 10 years after its revelation but never embraced Islam, even to try to 'prove' the Quran wrong.

questions.text: Is this a Makkan or Madinan Surah?
questions.answer: Makkan.

questions.text: How many verses are in Surah Al-Masad?
questions.answer: Five verses.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Abu Lahab'?
questions.answer: Father of the Flame (referring to his reddish complexion or his fate in Hell).

questions.text: What is 'Jidiha' in the final verse?
questions.answer: Her neck.

questions.text: Does this Surah prove that being a relative of a Prophet cannot save a person without faith?
questions.answer: Yes, Abu Lahab was the Prophet's own uncle.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Kasab' in this Surah?
questions.answer: What he earned (wealth, children, or status).

questions.text: How did Abu Lahab die?
questions.answer: He died of a repulsive disease (black smallpox) shortly after the Battle of Badr.

questions.text: What is the primary message of the Surah?
questions.answer: A severe warning against those who insult and hinder the message of Allah.
